where a large meadow is visible. A flat area in the west is alive with various
tall grasses and stands of wild wheat growing up out of the earth.
name: Yellow Flower Field

desc:
Situated just to the south of the ruins of some sort of dwelling, this bright
green meadow sits atop a raised section of ground. The elevation allows it to be
well watered by natural sources yet leaves it safe from the flooding that
appears to have turned the land to the east and far south into dense quagmires
of mud and rotting vegetation.
name: Sloping Meadow

desc:
One of many small grassy areas that are scattered about the region, this small
pasture is covered with a bright, green grass. It seems to be supplied by the
same underground source that makes the plains south of here into marshy ground.
Luckily for the grass, the entire area is raised on higher ground than the
surroundings.
name: Small Pasture

desc:
The land here appears to have once been cultivated as a garden, evidenced by the
regular rows of bright flowers that grow in geometric patterns. The ground is
raised as if a mole had burrowed perfectly straight, parallel lines running
north and south. To the east the ground slopes gently upwards towards a flat
area which is carpeted with bright green clover.
name: Red Flower Field

desc:
The mixture of goldenrod and honeysuckle that seems to grow abundantly just
north of here is sparse because the ground is rather unstable. Great upheavals
of dirt seem to have torn up any roots that were taking hold in the rocky soil.
Towards the east there is a noticeable transition towards very damp ground, and
an almost marshy surface.
name: A Golden Field

desc:
Large quantities of groundwater and deep mud make travel through this section
quite difficult. The mud seems to draw everything deeper into its depths. The
river which flows underground farther to the north must be the reason for the
water rising to the surface once again. Perhaps a natural spring once flowed
here, but there is no evidence of it now.
name: Marshy Plains

desc:
The ground grows softer and softer, but it does support your weight. Underfoot
it squelches, trying to drag you below. The tracks behind you fill quickly with
water. Rising all around are dense clumps of reeds, separated from each other by
small passageways of marshland.
name: Marshy Plains

desc:
The plant life grows lusher as it nears the water. Still no sound of a river can
be heard, although the air is moist. The incessant chirping of some small birds
can be heard in the distance and, nearer by, some small animal scurries into the
safety of the thick grass.
name: The Grassy Plains

desc:
This ancient trail is one of the few reminders of the civilisation that once
ruled these lands. Some small marker stones at the edges of the path can still
be seen, although many have been overgrown and lost amongst the grass. Not much
else can be seen except an ominous black tower rising above the horizon to the
west.
name: An Ancient Trail in the Plains

desc:
Once a field used by the inhabitants of the keep to grow crops of different
kinds, it has long since been overrun by weeds. Far west of it lie the remnants
of the ancient castle wall, which has fallen into disrepair. A small trail
skirts around the southern edge of the old field, heading west to the old castle
and east into the plains.
name: Old Fields

desc:
Once the ground was cleared here to prevent enemies from sneaking close to the
castle but now the high grasses and bushes have started to work their way back
towards the castle wall. Just west of you the old stone wall rises high above
the ground, still mighty and strong but aging and deserted. Its surface is
covered by vines and plants, giving it a peaceful and solemn look.
name: Plains by an Ancient Stone Wall

desc:
The thorns in the northwest have started to reclaim this old field where once
the keep's soldiers had their practice ground. What looks like old archery
targets lie broken and crumbled on the bare soil that still, after all these
years, bears marks of heavily burdened horses charging after imaginary foes. In
the east the old castle lies silent, still keeping watch on the old land.
name: An Old Practice Field

desc:
In front of you are the old castle gates, towering high above. Once they always
stood open, letting the farmers pass in and out with a mere guard to keep watch,
but now the gates are firmly shut and no sign of life can be seen. The walls are
covered by smaller plants and vines climbing towards the sky. The bushes have
crept up on the wall almost as if they wanted to sneak in and ambush the keep.
name: Before the Gates

desc:
Here the wall crumbled and fell. Great cracks yawn in the stonework and several
large stone blocks have fallen down, crushing the ground. Wild weeds and vines
have destroyed some of the larger stones, creating a landscape littered by
boulders of various sizes. Just skirting the havoc, the old trail passes on the
outside of the ruins of the wall, almost hidden from view, through the grass
striving towards the old castle.
name: An Old Trail

desc:
This old trail once ran into the fields that surrounded the keep, but now it has
fallen into oblivion. The plains have reclaimed the very ground that it used to
run on, and now the trail is cracked and overgrown, making it very hard to
follow.
name: An Overgrown Trail

desc:
The faint remnants of an old trail run through the plains here. Once
well-trodden, it is now nothing but a faint memory of an older era, when men
tamed these plains and grew crops on them. The crop fields were long ago
reclaimed by the plains, but the trail survives, the small marker stones lining
the way not yet completely overgrown.
name: A Forgotten Trail

desc:
The ground here is rather soft, and gives way a little as your weight is placed
on it. As you travel through the high grass, you manage to scare something that
rushes swiftly and noisily through the grass. Looking around, you see a high
hill far to your east, but elsewhere the landscape is just plains, stretching
towards the horizon.
name: Green Plains

desc:
The old fields here are now nothing but a memory as the ground is once again
covered in wild grasses and weeds. In a few places remnants of some old fences
can be spotted, but it takes quite a lot of imagination to see the old fields in
front of you.
name: Overgrown Fields

desc:
Remains of a small farmhouse can be discerned on the ground. Not much is left of
the walls, but some of the stonework of the hearth is still in place. Wooden
splinters lie scattered all around in the grass. A short distance north you see
the old castle rising above you, watching and guarding its old and undiscovered
secrets.
name: Remnants of a Farm

desc:
Passing through the centre of the ruined farm village the trail makes a turn
here, heading north towards the castle and west towards the horizon. Ruins of a
small farm lie just east of you and several overgrown fields lie scattered in
the area surrounding you.
name: An Unused Trail

desc:
The small path runs between old unused and overgrown fields from the old broken
road in the far west towards what once was a small farmer village. Small ruins
of houses can be seen around you as well as broken and rotted fences. A small
distance to your northeast lies the ancient castle that was the focal point of
the area in times of old.
name: A Path in the Plains

desc:
This small path runs alongside the thick thorns for quite a while, leading you
onward. South of the trail the grassy plains begin, stretching south and east
for what seems to be forever. The path leads east towards the old ruined keep,
and has obviously lain unused for many a year.
name: A Small Path

desc:
Movement at any speed here is a hardship, as the dense thorns tear and grab at
you. The rumbling of fast flowing water can be hear to the north, where the land
falls away in a huge drop down to the river. To the west the thorns are
impossible to pass, becoming so thick and dense that you cannot see through
them. East the thorns become sparser, and travel is easier there.
name: Dense Thornbushes

desc:
Thick dense thorns surround you, filtering out the sun, so that only a few
lonely rays of light manage to get down to the ground. In the everlasting dusk a
few small birds live and thrive, but the thorns are not a very friendly place
for larger animals. To the west and south there is a hedge of thorns that makes
those directions virtually impassable. East of here a path has been hacked
through the thorns, while northward the bushes become even thicker.
name: Thornbushes

desc:
The ground here is hard, well packed and far from perfectly covered by the
grasses and weeds. Brown spots of dirt show through the green and yellow sturdy
tufts of grass. The ground slowly slopes upwards as a rising cliff ascends to
the west, blocking sight farther westwards. North of here stands a small thicket
of thorns.
name: Harsh Plains

desc:
As if fallen down from the cliff to the north, the vegetation here consists of
high thorny bushes making it so difficult to ride or even walk here one would
wonder if they grew here on purpose. Small animals seem to thrive in the safe
haven the thorns provide, and noises from small birds can be heard from all
directions.
name: In the Low Thorns

desc:
The thorny bushes covering the land have all but exterminated any other kind of
flora. Coming from the west the thorns have climbed these cliffs to get more
sunlight. The steep drop to the south has not been an obstacle for the thorns
either, and thick tangles protrude creating the illusion of a sturdy overhang.
name: Thorny Cliff

desc:
This isolated field is covered in shrubs with a few trees scattered across it. A
small spring trickles into a tiny ribbon of water that flows to the river
bordering on the north. The high cliff south of here curves and blocks travel to
the east but is climbable in the south. Many layers of rock, each one of a
different colour, can be seen stretching in bands across the cliff wall.
name: Isolated Field

desc:
Tall grass covers this field in most places, though several circles have been
cleared and rocks moved so that simple makeshift fire pits could be created.
Scattered stumps show the origin of the rotting logs that were set as seats
around these fire pits. A cliff drops sharply at the north but the rock face
seems to be climbable.
name: Abandoned Camp

desc:
Around you stretches a field of rocks, covered in places with simple plants like
lichen and grass. The cliff to the north overlooking the river below also blocks
travel to the west. A soft rushing sound can be heard over the edge of the
northern cliff. The rocks here seem to settle deeper into the ground towards the
east and south, making it easier for plants to grow.
name: A Field above Two Cliffs

desc:
This field of grass, small trees and light brush stretches from the cliff in the
north into the south. The trees and brush grow thinner towards the edges of the
field. Small spots of rock thrust out of the ground, and grow more common to the
west.
name: Field above a Cliff

desc:
Surrounded by water to the north and west, with a cliff to the south and a
narrow field to the east, this field is somewhat isolated from the lands around
it. Small plants of various types struggle to live in the poor soil of this
field and are interspersed with pebbles.
name: Pebbled Field

desc:
This narrow field is nearly lifeless except for some patches of grass. It stands
below a cliff to the south and the river to the north. Rocks and boulders have
fallen off the cliff and lie here, making it hard for the plant life to find a
place with good soil. Some of the boulders have rolled into the river and make
the shoreline a big jagged. Plants begin to grow more common towards the wider
lands of the east, and the more narrow field to the west.
name: Narrow Field

desc:
This field is well watered by the river and well served by the silt the floods
bring. Grass, shrubs and a few trees crowd this land, with no dirt to be seen
anywhere, not even at the rivers edge. The southern cliff is well away from the
river, though it comes near the water in the western part of the field. Past the
eastern border of this field the ground becomes covered in deep patches of silt
that prevent any plants from growing except grass. In the far east a tower rises
on a mound overlooking this field.
name: Wide River Valley

desc:
Piles of silt litter this plain of grass. A rabbit could hop from pile to pile
and never trod on the grass lying between them. The rivers to the north and east
have natural levels, marked by a narrow shoreline lying just below the level of
this plain. Remnants of what might be ancient stone levees lie shattered near
the banks of the rivers. beyond the shattered stone and the river sits a mound
topped by a tower which can be easily seen from here.
name: Flood Plain

desc:
A lush field lies here, well watered by the runoff from the tall cliff that
rises along the western edge, and the water table resulting from the river to
the east. Tall grasses cover the field with a few large trees growing near the
rivers edge. To the south begin rows of stumps or posts, and northwards the
ground cover begins to die and small patches of silt lie scattered.
name: Lush Field

desc:
Cracks and small gullies make this field very hard to travel through. Many of
the cracks originate from the northern and eastern cliffs and the large western
crevice. The crevice running from the northwest to the southwest stretches too
far across for anything to jump, but the cracks in this field are generally less
than a foot wide and a few feet deep. A few large trees and some patches of
grass are all that grow in this field.
name: Cracked Field

desc:
This field is covered in light brush with large areas of grass. The lack of
large vegetation, such as trees, makes travel through here simple. The cliff
that drops to the north overlooks the river valley and appears cracked and
unstable. A crevice several paces wide runs from the cliff in the northeast
along the eastern of this field blocking all but birds.
name: Slightly Cracked Field

desc:
A large field full of small bramble patches makes travel through here a bit
difficult since paths through it must be used. These patches are offshoots of
the giant bramble patch to the south that have become isolated. A round section
of the southern wall of bramble is slightly discoloured.
name: Small Bramble Patches

desc:
A field of tall grass is here, almost two feet high in some places. Small trees
and high shrubs make it hard to see any great distance here. Anything on the
ground is impossible to see from more than a few feet anyway because of the
terrain and plant life.
name: Overgrown Field

desc:
This field of grass and small shrubs has several game trails going through it,
though most of them are sized for a rabbit or other small animals. These trails
go south, west and north through here. To the east of here is a large crevice
running along a north-south axis that is quite wide and impossible to cross.
name: A Field of Small Game Trails

desc:
A large circle of thirteen stumps and the fallen ancient oaks that spray
outwards from them dominate this field. An altar of granite sits in the centre
of the clearing, equal distance from each of the tree stumps. Small vines grow
up the side of the altar. A crevice runs along the west side of this field. To
the east begin posts or stumps set in rows.
name: Circle of Trees

desc:
Lines of stumps stand here, the remains of an orchard which has been hacked down
by axes and burned. The stumps are a few feet high and many have had their
hearts burned out. There is evidence of a deliberate burning of the core and
then a dousing when the surrounding part of the trunk started to burn. The
repeated burning of the cores has created hollow stumps of the former fruit
trees. The land grows more barren towards the east, with the other directions
showing more signs of life.
name: Destroyed Orchard

desc:
This former farmland is barren and dead, the earth has been sown heavily with
salt. No plants are growing here despite the irrigation from the old ditches
that lead to the river in the east and north. The old farm stretches to the
south and west. Beginning towards the west are what might be short posts
standing in rows.
name: Salted Farmland

desc:
Barren earth upon which nothing can grow makes this field very lifeless. A large
depression seems to have once been a pond. Ditches used to connect the
depression to the river in the east but they have been filled up with too much
loose blown earth to let water flow through them. Rotting wood and broken stone
in the ground show what might once been a farmhouse or a barn.
name: Barren Farmland

desc:
This meadow is mainly covered in tall grass though large patches of dead ground
exist where no life grows. The dead areas are mainly along the eastern and
northern edges of this meadow and the farther in those directions the larger the
patches become. Posts or stumps rise from a field to the north.
name: Patchwork Meadow

desc:
This meadow is covered in stalks of golden coloured wheat. There is no sign that
people have ever tried to cultivate this meadow, though the soil is rich and
fertile. The meadow is flat and stretches towards the horizon in each direction
except the north where a field with several stumps lies.
name: Meadow of Golden Wheat

desc:
Tall stalks of grass and wheat grow scattered in patches across this meadow.
Each patch is separated by small sections of bramble that keep the two grasses
separate and roughly equal in area. A large patch of brambles blocks travel to
the west. Tiny game trails at the northern edge run to and from this meadow.
name: Meadowland

desc:
This field has a number of small vegetable plots that have grown wild and no
longer grow in neat and orderly rows. Marks on the ground show where those rows
once were. A gigantic patch of thorny bramble stretches off into the distance in
the east.
name: Overgrown Field

desc:
A field of small tangled plants and grass lies here. The plants seem to be
trying to grow on top of each other in their struggle for light and good soil. A
few larger shrubs stand out among the other plants, but they themselves are
little more than two feet high, and only noticeable because of the shortness of
the other plants.
name: Tangled Field

desc:
Several varieties of green grass cover this field, each a different shade and
height. A tall, light green variety covers about half the field, but the other
types intermix with this variety. Fields continue to the north and east, while
flat grasslands stretch out in the south.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
A variety of garden plants, small trees and bushes grow in this field. Only a
little free room is left for travellers to move through except where grass is
growing by itself. There are enough grassy areas though that this field can be
walked, but not ridden, through.
name: Overgrown Field

desc:
A tall, thin, yellow grass reaches high towards the sky here. In many areas the
grass reaches over four feet in height. The density of the growth prevents a
quick look from seeing if any other plants are growing here hidden by the grass
or by a small depression in the ground.
name: Overgrown Grassland

desc:
This broad, grassy field has a number of mature trees in it but they are well
spread out across the field. Each tree is surrounded by a circle of brambles
about ten feet wide like a wall around a tower. The brambles are not spreading
since between the circles only tall grass exists.
name: Bramble Enclosed Trees

desc:
Clover covers this meadow in a deep, soft blanket. Only a few patches of tall,
green grass exist here scattered among the clover leaves. Two types of clover
compete here, a green clover and a much more prevalent white variety. The white
clover continues northwards but in the other directions grass dominates the
flora.
name: White Meadow

desc:
White and yellow dominate the colours of this meadow. The yellow comes from the
grass that encroaches from the east and north. White clover dominates most of
the meadow between the grassy areas and the brambles to the west. A few patches
of bramble lie scattered here, perhaps offshoots of the patch in the west.
name: White and Yellow Meadow

desc:
A light green grass covers the ground with its tall, wide-bladed stems. The tall
grass almost entirely prevents other species of grass and larger plants such as
bushes and trees from growing here. A few bushes have managed to push their tops
above the grass to receive sunlight, but they are deeply entangled in grass.
name: Grassland

desc:
This meadow of tall yellow grass has been overrun by low green shrubs. The very
vivid contrast between the colours makes it somewhat easier to find things here
since few animals are equipped to fade into both backgrounds. Grasslands and
meadows lie at the edges of this meadow, with a large campsite to the east.
name: Overgrown Meadow

desc:
Large areas have been cleared of all grass and brush in circles around one
hundred earthen fire pits. Little loose wood or other good material for a fire
is left here, only grass and some small shrubs cover the ground between where
the old campfires were. There is enough cleared area and fire pits prepared for
an army or many groups of travellers to camp here.
name: Large Campsite

desc:
This bank lies beside the river to the east and has a long gentle slope at the
edge making access to the river easy. The slope also makes it easy for the river
to come far inland when it floods. Small pieces of driftwood lie on the bank, or
float in small coves that protect them from being washed along by the rivers
flow. Anything floating in a cove can be reached quite easily from the riverbank
and the sides of the coves.
name: Riverbank

desc:
A few remaining posts still mark the boundaries of this dead pasture. Almost no
green or colour can be seen, except the brown of the soil. Small hills of dust
show where the wind has blown dried-up soil. Tiny chunks of salt exist in a few
places protected by posts from the weather. A river lies to the east but no
irrigation ditches or ponds are here to provide water to plants that attempt to
grow. Northwards is an equally barren land but more life shows towards the west
and south.
name: Dead Pasture

desc:
This field of tall grass has been left to grow, as it will for quite a while. A
few scattered small bushes and several small trees have managed to take root
here. The land grows barren towards the east and north. A meadow lies to the
west while a campsite is to the south.
name: Fallow Field

desc:
Yellow grass grows here, fighting the elements to stand straight and tall at all
times of the year and gain as much sunlight as possible. The grass can be easily
bent or broken since its stalks are not very strong unlike other types. In all
directions but the east stretch meadows. To the east the land is lush and
fertile.
name: Yellow Meadow

desc:
Sunken deep into the barren surroundings, a ravine opens up to the east. The
brown grass doesn't completely cover the dead soil on the steep slope down into
the ravine, and travelling there might be somewhat slippery. Small, thick and
hairy plants spot the landscape here, as well as scattered stone blocks.
name: Western Entrance to a Ravine

desc:
Two miles wide, this broad ravine cuts through the barren lands. The walls of it
are impossible to climb, and the few plants and trees that grow on them are
remarkably durable. The ravine bends and turns in various directions, making it
difficult to tell in what direction it leads. Birds can be seen flying in and
out from the ravine walls, where they probably have their small underground
nests. Once in a while a large bird of prey can be seen high up in the sky.
name: A Deep Ravine

desc:
Twisting and turning, the deep ravine keeps everyone without wings trapped on
the bottom of it. The ground is bare and only in a few places can brown grass be
seen. In the middle of the ravine there are clear signs of where an old brook
must have been, dried out now for many years. Old and dead plants are scattered
along the dried out brook.
name: A Deep Ravine

desc:
Grasses grow tall and dense, covering the fertile soil completely. The white and
green clover growing some distance to the north has lost its grip on this part
of the plains. Stretching out towards the horizon in every direction but west is
the hazy green of grass-covered plains. A deep ravine opens up west of here,
cutting through the lands creating an impassable obstacle.
name: Overgrown Grassland

desc:
Seemingly endless the high grasses dominate the surrounding area. The land seems
deserted except for some small animal tracks leading north towards a meadow. To
the south the grass seems more reluctant to grow and the land is more open.
name: Overgrown Grassland

desc:
Seemingly endless, high grasses dominate the surrounding view. The land is all
but deserted here, with the exception of some small animal tracks leading north
to what appears to be the remains of a large campsite. To the south even the
grass seems reluctant to grow, leaving that area even more open than here.
name: Overgrown Grassland

desc:
Reaching high above the land the cliff to the south is clearly unable to be
scaled. To the north the River Bruinen makes another natural border and thus all
travellers are for the most part restricted to journeying east or west. However,
judging from the lack of disturbance in the well-grown grass this area sees few
strangers.
name: Below the Cliff

desc:
The cliff comes to an abrupt end here as a tributary of the River Bruinen has
carved its way through the rock, blocking further passage. The fast flowing
waters swirl below the edge, sending echoes reverberating through the small
canyon. South of here a few low thorn bushes can be seen and westwards the
plateau looks to reach towards the horizon.
name: Above the Tributary

desc:
Several sturdy tufts of short and broad grasses grow upon this high plateau,
trying in vain to cover wind-ridden rock. In places where the soil has been
swept away by the fierce winds the dark rock shines through. To the west the
plateau expands, reaching toward a sharp edge and southwards it slopes gently
downwards. From the east one can hear the faint sound of a flowing river.
name: The High Cliff

desc:
This rocky plateau is sparsely covered with small tufts of sturdy grasses that
stand defiantly as the fierce winds howl by, ripping and tearing the very soil
upon which they grow. Southwards the plateau is completely covered by green
grasses while eastwards the rocks show through in a few of the barer spots.
name: On a Cliff

desc:
The plains of the west and the grasslands of the north come to an abrupt ending
here, as a vertical wall in the form of a cliff rises from the ground. The cliff
is clearly impossible to climb, lacking any recognisable hand or footholds. As
climbing is not an option travellers are forced to seek out reversible routes
further north or south.
name: At the Rock Wall

desc:
This grassland, devoid of any interesting landmarks, has suddenly, as if
correcting itself, sprung a tall cliff to the east. The shadow cast by this
earthen wall must cover miles each dawn. To the south the land rises some to
meet the wall, but it only serves to emphasise the contrast between the cliff
and the surrounding flat land.
name: Desolate Plain

desc:
Desolate and solemn, these grounds lie silent below the open sky. A large ravine
lies to the west and south, creating an insurmountable obstacle. The only sound
to be heard is the faint whisper of the wind as it gently causes large ripples
in the grass. Few animals can be seen in the dangerous open ground, as stealth
is the key to survival for many of the smaller animals inhabiting this dense
grassy area.
name: Desolate Plain

desc:
The eastern side of the valley floor slowly ascends until it finally meets the
wall of the ravine, where it makes a full stop. The slope has been travelled
recently, as several large tracks clearly show. A lonely tree grows on the
western slope, long since dead, and some of its old branches have already fallen
to the ground.
name: A Deep Ravine

desc:
The resilient green grass slowly grows more and more dense. Perhaps, sometime
long ago, other species of flora were represented here, but they have long ago
drowned in the sea of grass. The area grows denser to the north, maybe a sign of
more fertile grounds. A deep ravine opens up to the north and east, making
travelling in that direction impossible.
name: Grasslands

desc:
Resembling a large open sea the green grass of the plain here waves gently in
the passing winds, faint ripples rolling across the vast expanses of tall
straws. Reaching about a foot above the ground the sturdy grass covers
everything in sight, as well as reaching out towards the horizon.
name: Flowing Plains

desc:
The grass-covered lands clear up a bit here and you can barely discern faint
brown spots where the soil shines through. Even a few bushes and taller plants
have managed to get a hold here, although they are fighting a losing battle
against the sturdy grasses that stretch out in all directions as far as the eye
can see.
name: Open Plains

desc:
The vast desolate plains stretch out endlessly towards the horizon in all
directions, lying flat and unbroken as far as the eye can see. To the east a
deep ravine breaks the monotony, cutting deep through the barren landscape. The
grass covered ground almost resembles a large expanse of water, rolling as the
wind gently caresses the sea green surface, causing rippling waves throughout
the vast ocean of greenery.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
The ravine continues its way, cutting deep into the barren lands of this part of
Arda. Stone blocks and dead plants cover the uneven bottom of the ravine, as
well as clear signs of an old dried out brook. The ground is muddy at intervals,
showing signs of the brook being filled with water at times.
name: A Deep Ravine

desc:
The steep walls of this deep ravine make any sound reverberate between them and
echo for a few seconds before finally coming to an end. This part of the ravine
is devoid of life, and the only proof that life once existed here is the
collection of small dead plants that have failed their struggle for survival in
the stony soil.
name: A Deep Ravine

desc:
The ground here seems to surge up from the surrounding plains, ending at a steep
wall to the east. Otherwise the uninterrupted view spans several miles in all
other direction. The deep ravine to the west makes a grand view. Even still,
spotting movement would be difficult due to the patches of different tinged
grass and their constant motions caused by the passing winds.
name: Grassy Slope

desc:
Lying in shadow for most of each day, the grasses here grow rather sparse and
low. Bleak fragile weeds cling to the ground in a vain attempt to suck what
little nourishment they can out of the barren earth. Some of the passing winds
howl across the cliff's edge high above while others whistle as they blow down
the side of the cliff.
name: At a Sheer Cliff Wall

desc:
The plains are cut in two as the high cliff wall rises ominously into the air,
creating an eighty foot drop just to the west. This plateau is covered in short
and sturdy grasses which thrive despite the fierce winds howling by, ripping and
tearing at the soil. To the north the cliff is rather bare, the stone showing
through the grasses, while eastwards the grass grows stronger as the plains
slightly slant downwards.
name: High Plateau

desc:
Hindered by natural barriers of various kinds, it is unlikely this plateau would
be accessible by many animals. The serene environment is reminiscent of a farmed
meadow. The only kind of life here is that which could and would traverse the
various obstacles to get here.
name: Grassy Plateau

desc:
The grasslands abruptly come to an end to the east as the soil of the cliffs
lacks the nourishment for the grasses to thrive. More resilient vegetation, such
as thorn bushes, cling to every crevice and crack in the cliffs. While these
cliffs are scaleable, it would surely be a taxing and unpleasant experience.
name: Vanishing Thorns

desc:
Stretching endlessly towards the hazy line where green meets blue the tall broad
grasses grow carelessly here, completely enveloping the fertile soil in a dense
green carpet. Moving slowly back and forth as the wind gently rolls in from the
distant misty mountains the grass whispers gently in the breeze.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
The structure of the plains changes gradually as the grasslands span these lands
from north to south. To the south the plains lie rich and green, filled with the
sounds and smells of life. Northward the plains become more desolate and void of
animals. Even the colour of the plains slowly loses its depth as the grasses
turn bleak to the north. Just east of here there stands a small cluster of young
trees and westward the plains seem to reach on infinitely.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
Breaking the monotony of the surrounding flat terrain the trees form a rough
circle, clustering together as if they were looking for protection in numbers.
To the north and east the trees grow all the way to the edge of a deep ravine.
Many small animals seem to have taken refuge here from the dangerously open
lands around and judging from the faint noises the fauna is thriving.
name: A Cluster of Trees

desc:
Some places seem to mysteriously attract moisture, but this place is just the
opposite; every little breeze makes the dust swirl up, creating mini tornadoes
that travel across the bottom of the ravine. Lacking vegetation that binds the
dust to the ground, the sometimes steep slopes of this ravine are slippery to
tread. The ravine comes to an end just south of here, climbing up towards the
level of the surrounding barren lands.
name: A Deep Ravine

desc:
In these far-reaching plains the dominant feature is the never ending grass
growing unhindered in all directions, north to south and east to west. Small
insects and tiny birds fill the air, but the ground is less lively since the
grass provides no hiding place for even small animals. No large predators would
be able to make a life feeding solely around here. To the west a large ravine
opens up, making travelling in that direction impossible.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
The rolling plains stretch out without doubt or hesitation, reaching for the
distant horizon in all directions. The only inconsistency in these grass-covered
lands is the cliff wall rising high a few miles northeast of you. Whispering
softly in the wind the grass speaks of ages of old and of a history it can sense
through its roots.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
This dry cave digs deep into the sandy, and sometimes stony, soil. The exit,
that lies in the western wall, leads out into a ravine. The floor is uneven, and
in some places stones rise up from the floor, creating obstacles for anyone
traversing this cave. The cave is obviously inhabited, as several bones from
dead animals are scattered all around.
name: A Dry Cave

desc:
The curved rock wall lies in shadow most of the day, making it look smooth and
dark. The ground below is only partly covered in grass and the brown dry soil
can be seen shining through the bleak green grass. The grass grows more fertile
and lush the further away it is from the towering cliff wall.
name: Dark Rock Wall

desc:
Somewhat flatter than the surrounding areas, this place resembles a farmed
meadow with strictly defined borders formed by thorn bushes and cliffs. This
place could probably support several families of large animals, but its
inaccessibility probably deters them from settling here. Crowning the plateau
sits a small copse of trees.
name: Grassy Plateau

desc:
Climbing slowly as it travels north, the rising cliff is not very steep and can
carry the grass-covered soil well on its surface. Farther north the ground
levels out into an open plateau covered in a hazy greenery. Towards the south
the vast open plains roll out as far as the eye can see.
name: Rising Cliff

desc:
Here in the almost ever-present shadow of the cliff wall the grasses are not as
dominant as in the surrounding areas. The few tufts of grass that try to live
here are short and weak. The cliff wall climbs higher as it travel northwest
from here and descends to the level of the plains further south.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
What appeared to be plains land with no end comes here to an abrupt stop. To the
north a dark, ominous wall of granite effectively ends the plains. The shadow
cast by the wall is the favourite resting place for all kinds of animals judging
by the well trampled ground.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
The sturdy green grass continues seamlessly from horizon to horizon with the
lone exception of far to the northeast, where some kind of high hill or small
mountain can be seen. The land looks to be devoid of life, but with such an
unobstructed view of the surrounding area any creatures living here would have
had ample time to hide from your approach. For now, though, even the blue sky
seems deserted.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
Flat, monotonous plains stretch from horizon to horizon. The short grass
covering everything does nothing to break the uniform looks of this place and as
the only source of food for animals, there is not exactly an abundance of life
milling around. Small insects and an occasional rabbit might find sustenance
here.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
Swiftly changing from dry dust covered grass in the north to richer fed grass in
the damp south, these plains sprawl unhindered in all directions. Most of the
animals dwelling here are of the smaller sort, at least those that dare enter
the open ground. This is a place where the wind rules along with the dust it
carries.
name: Vast Grassy Plains

desc:
The short resilient grass covers most of the area and with the sole exception of
a small cluster of trees to the north this type of vegetation continues in all
directions. To the south the grass grows denser, and the ground seems moister.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass covered plains stretch out towards the horizon in all directions. The
thick and high grass covers and hides the soil from the sky and provides shelter
for several small animals which can be heard scurrying about in the dense
vegetation. High above small black shapes can be seen, riding the wind currents
and searching for prey.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass-covered plains stretch endlessly towards the hazy horizon, as if
slowly reaching out for the thin line where sky and land meet. The knee-high
grasses sway slowly in the passing breezes that make the area roll and billow
like a wide ocean, its waves slow and unstoppable, travelling without rest or
sleep across the surface.
name: Grassland

desc:
Weather and wind over time have taken their toll, eroding the land. Nowadays
these gently rolling hills are bordered by roads to the east and south, and to
the west some small trees or high bushes cover the land.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Small and thin trees fight for sun and space as they break through the hold of
the grass here. Creating a small sparse thicket they have won a short respite as
they now manage to shadow a small part of the plains. Within the branches of the
low trees the sounds of small animals moving about can be heard as they hide
carefully among the shadows.
name: A Sparse Thicket

desc:
As if they continue on to the sea, these monotonous plains stretch forth in all
directions. Their journey appears almost ceaseless with only a minor
interruption by a few scattered bushes that grow a ways to the east, struggling
valiantly to compete with the vigorous grasses.
name: Open Plains

desc:
Open space surrounds this area and the only moving thing visible is the grass,
swaying in the passing winds. The place is hospitable enough for some large
animals, but if some exist the grass effectively covers all tracks of them.
name: Grassland

desc:
Soft and spongy the ground is clearly getting its share of water. The lush
grasses grow high and strong, reinforcing the fact that there is ample, but
still not an over abundance of, moisture contained within the soil. A small
distance to the south you can hear a small spring babbling. This sound blends in
with the soft noises of the passing winds blowing across the vast surrounding
plains.
name: Plains

desc:
This old field has gradually given way to the water, slowly becoming drenched
and marshy. Large tufts stand high above the soft ground and the grasses are
dark green in colour. The ground below sucks and drags upon the weight of
travellers, causing bubbles to reach the surface of the soggy soil. Each step
sends the stench of rotting vegetation pouring into the air.
name: Wetlands

desc:
This ancient field often lies drenched in water, drowning the green grasses as
the stream just south of here floods its banks. Small pools of dark and murky
water stand still in various depressions in the field, emitting the stench of
rotting vegetation. Further north the grasses lie on higher ground, out of the
waters reach, and thus grow healthier. Further east fields lie drowned by the
stream for years, creating a small swamp in the midst of the lush grasslands. To
the west there lies a ruined old farm.
name: Wet Field

desc:
In the middle of these old fields stands the cracked and blackened remains of an
old farmhouse. This decaying framework is what is left of this once large and
well-built structure. The wood is cracked and splintered, as well as almost
completely overgrown by sturdy weeds that try to lay claim to the area. The
grassy plains reach north and west, stretching endlessly towards the distant
horizon. Just south of here there sparkles a small river.
name: A Ruined Farm

desc:
The plains slope downwards here as a small river runs wildly westward just south
of you. The babblings of the rushing water mix with the chirping of hidden birds
as well as the scratching and scraping of small mammals running through the
dense undergrowth. The plains stretch out towards the horizon without end in all
directions, lying flat and almost completely unbroken for miles and miles, but
just east of here you can spot some small structure rising above the grass.
name: River Bank

desc:
The deep river rushes swiftly westwards, frothing and cascading as it travels
towards some distant goal far away in the west. The passage south is blocked by
a rocky wall, carved out by the river throughout the ages and the northern bank
slopes steeply upwards, making the journey in that direction arduous, but still
accessible.
name: Turbulent Waters

desc:
Running between ruined farms and old, overgrown pasturelands this water courses
on rapidly westwards, picking up pace as the river grows deeper and narrower.
The slopes of the riverbanks not so steep as to prove an obstacle for the
seasoned traveller, although the incline of the grassy riverbanks could prove
taxing for the weary or the weak.
name: Rapid River

desc:
The once small stream starts picking up its pace as the water rushes westward,
running between old fields and pastures. The banks of the stream are not very
steep, nor very high, thus leaving one's view almost completely unobstructed in
all directions. To the south an old landing can be seen, lying abandoned and
broken on the bank. Northwards lie wet fields.
name: Swift Waters

desc:
The small stream runs westwards, slowly picking up pace as it is continually fed
water from the surrounding wetlands. It travels carefully between the old
overgrown pastures and old drenched fields, babbling and sparkling as it seeks
the adventure of a long and rough journey.
name: A Babbling Stream

desc:
Bubbling out of the ground amid the grassy plains, this small spring is the
centre of daily life for many small animals. Supplying the surrounding
grasslands with water the small spring is also the source of a minor stream
journeying westwards, dividing some long unused farmer's fields and once tamed
pastureland.
name: A Small Spring

desc:
Tough green grasses totally engulf this place making even the various hardy
weeds compete for their survival. This place could easily feed even large
herbivores, but strangely there seems to be a lack of them here. The stench of
what remains of some large carnivorous creatures kill rises from spots where the
grass has turned yellow-brownish under the scant animal remains.
name: Grassland

desc:
Open plains stretch out for what seems to be forever with only some small
differences in hue to the west and a faint sign of trails or maybe even a road
to the southeast. The monotony of the place would surely drive more intelligent
creatures insane if forced to dwell here but the small amount of wildlife seems
content.
name: Open Plains

desc:
Underfoot the ground is hard and cracked, the remnants of an old trail still a
part of the landscape. While still visible, the path is slowly being reclaimed
by the sturdy grasses and plants that grow on the plains that open up towards
the western horizon. To the south the path runs towards the rising cliff wall
and eastwards the trail reaches out for an abandoned road that appears only as a
thin line in the greenery.
name: Abandoned Trail

desc:
This small cave in the ground is littered by debris; a few scraps of wood, a
small boulder and several carcasses of unfortunate animals. The walls of the
cave are roughly cut and entirely undecorated.
name: A Small Earth Chamber

desc:
The small passage twists downwards in an endless slope, smoothly cut through the
rock and dirt. Strange echoes and voices are carried by the walls and small
spots of light flicker in and out of existence.
name: A Sloping Passage

desc:
At the bottom of the sloping corridor a small chamber opens up. The walls are
smoothly cut out of the rock and the floor is packed dirt. On the walls there
are several inscriptions and carvings that surround replicas of a red eye. On
the ground in the centre of the room stands a small black altar.
name: A Dark Altar

desc:
Close to the edge of the cliff the grass still grows thick and dense, not caring
about the eighty-foot drop to the plains below. The plateau reaches further
north along the edge of the cliff as well as sloping gently downwards as it
travels towards the east.
name: Edge of the Plateau

desc:
The grass grows rather short here, being so close to the old road that runs
through the plains as if someone cut the ground with a knife. Not much disturbs
the peace here, an occasional bird or some small animal rustling through the
thicker undergrowth. The sharp line indicating the road lies a bit to your west
and north, while the thick green expanses of grass cover the soil all around
you.
name: Plains by the Road

desc:
Enveloped by the high grass, you are surrounded by the eternal struggle for
survival. Small birds fly in mystical patterns in the sky, chasing creatures
invisible to you. Sounds from crawling animals can be heard from all directions
as the smaller inhabitants make their way through the grass. Around you the
plains stretch out like a huge green blanket, covering everything that you can
see.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
Here used to lie an old field, but the farmers that used to live here are hardly
remembered by the soil these days. The once so rich fields are now grass-covered
and several kinds of weeds grow wild here. Rotting poles of wood can be spotted
in a few places, indicating where the old fences used to be. This old reclaimed
field now bears every likeness to the vast, surrounding ocean of grass.
name: An Old, Overgrown Field

desc:
The field extends all the way up to the old castle wall. Once burned to keep it
clear so the keep's inhabitants would be alerted of approaching enemies, it is
now, once again, covered in grass and weeds. The wall blocks all passage to the
north, but nothing prevents you from going elsewhere. Some kind of ruin lies
dead east, and on all other sides the plains flow endlessly.
name: An Old Field

desc:
Not much is left of this old farm. The walls and the roof caved in, leaving
nothing but a few rotting logs intact. Everything else is now covered by the
grass of the plains growing in on every side, as nature strives to reclaim what
was once in its possession.
name: Ruins of a Farmhouse

desc:
This old vineyard has now grown wild. Weeds, grasses, and vines compete for the
soil, and the match is even so far. Remains of a fence litter the ground, and
just west of here you spot some old ruins. The flowing plains surround you on
all the other sides.
name: An Old Vineyard

desc:
You travel on a part of the plains that connects the old overgrown fields of the
nearby abandoned keep with the dried grass south and the lush green grounds to
the north and east. You can spot a high hill due east, it being one of the most
prominent landmarks here. Far to the northwest you spot a tower.
name: Flowing Plains

desc:
A spacious cavern opens inside the ancient hill. It is shrouded in darkness and
silence reigns, yet the atmosphere is warm and sultry. The walls are of bare
rock, with sharpened edges and angular shapes, and the ground seems uneven and
unsafe. A closer inspection reveals deep scratches and shreds of thick hair
lying on the ground, unequivocal sign that some large beast inhabits this big
grotto.
name: A Recondite Cave

desc:
This small, well hidden trail leads through the bushes to the tower, which rises
above the thorns. The large bushes create an impenetrable wall around you,
almost like a tunnel of green and brown into which the sun only partly reaches.
name: A Hidden Trail

desc:
It might be possible to reach the ledge below, but the straight course could
well prove to be fatal. There is a magnificent view from this landmark and many
paths that may reach firmer ground can be spotted. Some routes may prove to be
dead-ends or passages through which there is no return, so a traveller in these
lands should be wary and careful before setting out on a journey.
name: Above a Ledge

desc:
This large cave has two sections, the western half here is dominated by a large
pool of water running from the west wall to a stone dock on the edge of the
stone floor in the eastern cave. The roof and walls of the cave are all made of
dark blocks of stone with the cracks filled by mortar. The floor of this part of
the cave might also be artificial but it lies too far below the surface of the
pool to see.
name: Watery Cave

desc:
Dark stone blocks that have been fitted together closely make up the floor, roof
and walls here. This part of the large cave is full of the remains of shelves,
barrels, and other commonly used methods of storing materials. The wood has
decayed and rotted in the moist air over time. The edge of a large pool of water
lies on the west edge of this section of the cave. In the east wall is an area
about the size of a door that has filled with debris from what might be a
collapsed tunnel beyond.
name: Storage Cave

desc:
The living pressure of the River Bruinen has gnawed away at the countryside and
formed this calm basin adjacent to the river. The slower water makes it a
popular place for the animals of the land to gather and drink. The water is
murky from the settling mud brought down from the water to the north; the murky
colour makes it impossible to tell what could be found deep within its depths.
name: At the Basin

desc:
The river bends sharply here as it flows from the east to the north. White foam
and bubbles can be seen in the swift water. Branches and other flotsam float
along the western edge of the river. All the flotsam is heavily damaged or
broken into small pieces.
name: Sharp Bend in the Tributary River

desc:
The tunnel runs south through this large field of brambles, which is miles in
width. The brambles grow over three feet high in most places. This tunnel is
made up of a natural ravine several feet below the surrounding ground with the
brambles directly above the tunnel growing in a foot thick arch over it. The
thin layer of brambles is not enough to prevent weather and light from reaching
through. At the north end of the tunnel stretches a bramble wall in which is a
slightly discoloured, circular patch.
name: Bramble Tunnel

desc:
This room is partly above ground and partly dug out of the earth. From the hard
packed earth floor to the bramble roof is about ten feet with several of them
above the fields ground level. The bramble roof and upper walls have been
plugged by leaves and dirt to prevent light coming in or out of this hidden
room, except though special spy holes with views of the surrounding lands.
name: Dugout Room in the Brambles

desc:
The wet ground looks as if someone took a giant fist and pushed down the surface
to create a large sunken depression, or perhaps it was mined out from underneath
by someone. To the west a slope leads upward to some sort of ruins. Southward
the mud and water mix to form a viscous quagmire. It looks quite unstable, but
one could easily enter it from here.
name: Marshy Plains

desc:
Broken stonework lies scattered across the grassy plains, as the once so proud
and well-maintained road has fallen into oblivion. Large cracks scar the small
remnants of almost unbroken road that still can be seen above the thick cover of
dirt and grass.
name: Abandoned Broken Road

desc:
Hidden below a dense carpet of grass as well as a thick layer of soil, the
remnants of this once important trade route to Khazad-dum can barely be seen.
The broken slabs of stone, once so carefully transported and fitted to form this
road, now lie cracked and broken below the triumphant grasses that have
overwhelmed them.
name: Abandoned Broken Road

desc:
Covered by dust and weeds, the ground below you is most definitely the remains
of an old road. Now it is forgotten and broken, but some signs that it was not
always so can still be seen. Barely discernible beneath the soil are the huge
slabs it was so skilfully paved with many centuries ago, cleaving a passage
through the plains. Close to the north grow several dense thickets of thorns,
amidst which rises a dark tower.
name: Abandoned Broken Road

desc:
Branching off the once so proud road, this small path was once well used and
well tended. Now the surrounding plains are slowly reclaiming it, but the ground
is still hard, cracked and dry. Opening up towards the northwest the vast plains
roll on endlessly towards the horizon and just south of here there rises a bare
cliff wall.
name: Deserted Path
